``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined For general-rules people in prison, first eligibility for discretionary parole release occurs at the 25-percent mark of incarcerated people's judicial maximum sentences. Sentencing courts in Montana have the unusual power to order in individual cases that people in prison will never enjoy eligibility for discretionary release or that their minimum terms to release eligibility will be delayed past the 25-percent mark of their maximum sentences. Montana is one of a small number of states that have abolished good time, earned time, and related credits for people in prison to reduce their judicial maximum sentences or the period of time to first parole-release eligibility.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were 488 people in prison past their parole consideration year. This group made up 49% of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
### Demographics The demographics of people in prison past their parole consideration year are mostly notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and Other race(s), non-Hispanic people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
### Criminal Offenses ::: {.cell} ::: In 2020, 62% of people in prison past their parole consideration year were in prison for violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses reveals a more varied landscape, with most people incarcerated for aggravated or simple assault (26%) and rape or sexual assault (19%) offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
### Original Sentence Lengths In 2020, most people in prison past their parole consideration year had original sentence lengths between 10 to 24.9 years representing 61% of those eligible for parole. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Missing Data This section will contain information related to missingness in the NCRP data. Keep?


# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population increased 16%, changing from 3,716 in 2010 to 4,313 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners Series
## Demographics The demographics of people in prison are mostly notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and Other race(s), non-Hispanic people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners 2022 - Race and Ethnicity
## Criminal Offenses In 2020, 66% of people in prison were incarcerated for violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses reveals a more varied landscape, with most people incarcerated for rape or sexual assault (27%) and aggravated or simple assault (17%) offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Sentence Lengths In 2020, most people in prison had original sentence lengths between 10 to 24.9 years representing 36%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases decreased 5%, dropping from 1,206 in 2010 to 1,144 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
In 2020, 39% of people eligible for parole were released during their eligibility year. This represents a 34% increase compared to 2010. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison are mostly notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and other race(s), non-hispanic people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense Between 2010 and 2020, there were shifts in the average time served by individuals for different offense types in Montana. The largest change was for other or unknown offenses, which decreased by 77%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

## Years Spent in Prison After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity In 2020, people in prison past their parole eligibility year collectively spent a total of 331 years behind bars instead of supervised in the community on parole.

## Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender In progress. Same type of graph as race and ethnicity tab. :::
## Time Served ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: White people faced the longest average time served in prison in 2020, with an average of 1.9 years. Hispanic people experienced shorter prison stays, averaging 1.2 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: By offense type, disparities were observed in time served by race and ethnicity. For other violent offenses offenses, Black people had 2.3 more years on average compared to Hispanic people, who had the shortest time served for these offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender In progress. Same type of graph as race and ethnicity tab. :::
